raw_router_outputs.text: I put together a plan to move our whole team off Jira onto Linear next sprint. Here it is — tell me honestly, is it any good?
Drafted a cold outreach email to a potential investor. Can you look it over and give me your take on whether it lands?
I've decided we should drop the free tier entirely and go paid-only. Convince me I'm not making a mistake here.
Thinking of rewriting our billing service in Rust for the performance win. Sound like a smart move to you?
Here's my argument for why we should skip a Series A and bootstrap to profitability instead. What's your honest read on it?
We're about to launch the new checkout flow Friday. Before we ship, walk me through every way this could blow up on us.
We're at a fork: commit to a multi-year AWS reserved-instance contract, or stay on-demand. Is this the kind of call we can walk back later, and how big a deal is it if we're wrong?
Can you help me figure out the regex to capture everything between two curly braces in a config file, non-greedy?
What's the population of Yerevan and roughly how far is it from Tbilisi by car?
Honestly I'm just feeling stuck and burned out on this project and could use someone to talk it through with — not looking for anyone to poke holes in it.
I've decided to bundle onboarding and support into one paid tier for my SaaS. Tell me straight — does this hold up, or am I about to walk into a wall?
Talk me into shutting down our free plan and going paid-only. I want to hear the case for it before I pull the trigger.
My plan is to migrate the whole team off Slack to a self-hosted chat server this quarter. Poke holes in it and turn every hole into something the plan has to handle.
I'm set on raising our seed round at a $12M cap. Give me the list of what would have to be true for this to blow up, so I can make sure I've closed each one off.
We're going to open a second warehouse in Poland to cut EU shipping times. Assume you were trying to sabotage this — what would you exploit, and what does that tell me I need to lock down?
We ship the new checkout flow to production Friday. Before it goes live, walk me through the ways this ends up as a disaster three months from now and how likely each one is.
I believe remote-first is strictly better than hybrid for our engineering org. Build the strongest possible argument for the opposite view and hold it against me even if I push back.
My Postgres query that joins orders to line_items runs fine on staging but times out in prod. Can you help me figure out why and rewrite it?
Can you give me a weeknight recipe for salmon that uses miso and takes under thirty minutes?
I'm still figuring out how to grow our newsletter — spin up a bunch of different directions we could take, from referral loops to niche partnerships, so I have options to look at.
